ES1! · CME · PLAN FOR JUL 08/09, 2026 · BUILT WED JUL 08 ~17:50 ET

ES Trading Plan — Wed ETH & Thu RTH

ES last 7,518.25 (post-close, −8.5 off the 7,526.75 RTH settle, sitting on the daily 20-EMA 7,517.25) · Wed RTH closed 7,526.75 Inside VA > POC (VAL 7,499.50 / POC 7,521.25 / VAH 7,537.00, value shifted LOWER a 2nd day) after a 46-pt gap-down open drove to a Poor Low 7,469.50 against the daily P-shelf 7,474.00 (1.101M) and reversed — leaving a Poor High 7,536.50 too: both extremes unfinished · Overhead: the 7,537→7,543 value-gap seam (Poor High + Wed VAH → yVAL), then yPOC 7,566.25 / yVAH 7,571.50 + drawn 7,565.00, Selling Tail 7,588.00, and the 7,600.50–7,605.50 weak-high/POC supply block · Must-hold: 7,499.50 VAL, then the 7,469.50–7,474.00 fork; deep floor 7,439.25–7,455.50 (50-EMA + Buying Tail 7,443.50 + Prev VAL) · Master catalyst: Iran — US strikes resumed during this ETH, oil +5%, feeding a hike-risk Fed (minutes: some officials see a hike case; claims 8:30 Thu, CPI Jul 14) · VX1! 18.20 (18.90 rejected twice) · ATR 94.25 · MOC imbalance not provided — unweighted.
ES 7,518.25 — on the 20-EMA 7,517.25 gate Wed value 7,499.50 / 7,521.25 / 7,537.00 · shift LOWER · close > POC Structure: Poor High 7,536.50 + Poor Low 7,469.50 — double-unfinished bracket Floor: 7,469.50–7,474.00 shelf (Poor Low + daily P 1.101M) · deep 7,439–7,456 cluster Overhead: 7,537–7,543 seam → 7,565–7,571.50 → tail 7,588 → 7,600–7,605 block VX1! 18.20 flat · 18.90 rejected ×2 · cash VIX 16.90 +4.8% Iran strikes LIVE tonight · hike-risk Fed · ATR 94.25
POST-CLOSE RE-ANCHOR (Wed Jul 08, ~17:50 ET) — strikes resumed during ETH; both profile extremes left unfinished Wednesday gapped down 46 pts (0.49×ATR, with Tuesday's weak close), open-drove from 7,507 to the 7,469.50 Poor Low — tagging the daily P-shelf at 7,474.00 (1.101M volume node) — then look-below-and-failed: an initiative V-recovery reclaimed 7,482.25 / 7,500 / 7,508.25 and pushed to a Poor High 7,536.50 before settling 7,526.75, back above the 7,521.25 POC (Inside VA > POC). Value migrated lower a second straight day (7,543–7,571.50 → 7,499.50–7,537.00) but close location fought back — sellers had a live war headline and could not get acceptance below the shelf. Since the bell ES has bled ~8 pts to 7,518.25 as the US resumed strikes on Iran ("finish the job") — the tape is headline-driven all night. Master variable: Iran escalation vs de-escalation, feeding a hike-risk Fed (today's minutes: neutral two-way stance, a minority sees a hike case; oil +5% WTI ~$74; claims 8:30 Thu is minor, June CPI Jul 14 is the next true binary — an escalation that spikes oil is a SELL in this regime, a de-escalation headline is a BUY). The plan: acceptance above 7,537.00–7,543.00 (poor-high repair + value-gap fill) validates the recovery and triggers runs to 7,550.50 → 7,565.00–7,571.50 (fade zone unless de-escalation is real); failure below 7,499.50 re-opens the 7,469.50–7,474.00 fork — reclaim = dip-buy long, 30-min acceptance below = trend-down to 7,455.50 → 7,439–7,448. MOC imbalance not provided — stated unweighted. ETH bias: 30% Bull / 37% Neutral / 33% Bear — a headline coin-flip with a slight negative drift already printing, held up only by Wednesday's responsive close and a contained VIX. Date + catalyst reconciled via search (Jul 8 close: SPX −0.28%, Dow −1.09%, Nasdaq +0.20%; FOMC minutes 2:00 ET; strikes confirmed post-close).

01Top-Down Analysis — Overnight (Wed Jul 08 ETH → Thu 9:30 open)

The overnight reads against Wednesday's completed RTH profile until an ON value area develops. Everything below is anchored to the 7,499.50 / 7,521.25 / 7,537.00 value frame, the double-unfinished extremes, and a live-headline tape.

a) Daily — full bull stack, but the trend gate is under price tonight

Daily: 20-EMA 7,517.25 exactly at price · full rising stack below · P-shelf 7,474.00 (1.101M) under Wed's poor low · ATH 7,694.75 · ATR 94.25
MAValueDistance (vs 7,518.25)Status
20-EMA7,517.25+1.00ON THE LINE the trend gate — Wed's daily close 7,519 held it by <2 pts; Thursday's close decides
50-EMA7,439.25+79.00HOLDING rising — sits inside the 7,439–7,456 deep-floor cluster
100-EMA7,296.25+222.00HOLDING rising
200-EMA7,091.25+427.00HOLDING rising
300-EMA6,921.00+597.25HOLDING rising
Daily drawn levelTypeDistanceNote
7,694.75ATH / High+176.50All-time high — the ceiling of the whole structure
7,474.00P — volume shelf (1.101M)−44.25🔗 Directly under Wed's 7,469.50 poor low — together they ARE the fork
7,277.00V — value node (58K)−241.25Thin node near the 100-EMA; only relevant on a regime break
6,943.75 / 6,750 / 6,699 / 6,584.75 / 6,467.25Lower P/V ladder−574 and belowDeep composite structure — out of play for this plan
Why the 20-EMA gate matters tonightEvery dip since April has been bought at or above this rising line — it is the mechanical definition of "the dip-buy regime is intact." Price is sleeping ON it with strikes underway. An overnight probe below it means nothing (the gate is a CLOSING-basis test), but it tells you where the algos' line in the sand sits: sustained ETH trade below ~7,517 pulls the 7,499.50 VAL test forward, and a Thursday daily close below it would be the first closing loss of the gate in this leg — that, not an overnight wick, is what flips the daily trend read.
Why the 7,474.00 P-shelf matters1.101M contracts of composite volume built a ledge exactly where Wednesday's flush stopped. That is not coincidence — responsive buyers defended the shelf with a war headline in their face. It is now a twice-loaded level: an unfinished poor low ABOVE it wants repair (magnet), and the shelf UNDER it is where the auction decides whether the repair is a look-below-and-fail (buy) or the start of acceptance into the vacuum toward 7,455.50 → 7,421.25 (sell). Treat 7,469.50–7,474.00 as one decision zone, never a simple level.

Narrative: the daily trend is unambiguous — a fully stacked, rising 20>50>100>200>300 EMA ladder, price 2.3% off the 7,694.75 ATH. What has changed is location and regime: three sessions have compressed price back onto the 20-EMA while oil, a hike-tilted Fed, and a shooting war press on the tape. Wednesday's candle is constructive in isolation (gap down, long lower shadow off the shelf, close near the top of the body) but it printed INSIDE a two-day lower-value migration. The daily says: uptrend until the gate closes below, with the burden of proof shifting to buyers for the first time since the spring.

b) 5-min price-action recap + open type — the gap-trap reversal, minute by minute

5-min Jul 8: gap-down 7,507 open → A-flush to Poor Low 7,469.50 (10:20) → V-recovery through 7,482.25/7,508.25 → 12:30 push stopped at Poor High 7,536.50 → afternoon balance → settle 7,526.75 → post-close drift 7,518.25 on strike headlines
Time (ET)BeatOrder-flow read
04:00–08:30ETH pressed 7,470–7,485, recovered to ~7,515 into the open windowOvernight sellers (Samsung/chips hangover + oil) probed the shelf FIRST in the thin session and already failed once — first clue the level was real
09:30–09:45Gap-down open 7,507 (−46 vs Tue 7,553 close, 0.49×ATR, with the weak-close signal); immediate drive to ~7,482Open-drive DOWN — initiative sellers pressing the gap; gap-and-go classification live at the bell
10:15–10:30Second leg to 7,469.50 — flat low, no excess tail — and the flush diesThe drive hit the 7,474.00 P-shelf and found a wall of resting bids; VIX printed its 18.90 high on this exact bar and was REJECTED — sellers exhausted with maximum news tailwind
10:45–12:30Initiative V-recovery: 7,482.25 reclaimed, 7,500 and 7,508.25 taken without pullback, push extends to 7,536.50Look-below-and-fail confirmed — shorts trapped below 7,500 covering into responsive+initiative buying; the speed of the climb means the 7,482–7,508 path is thin (fast-repricing zone both ways)
12:30–15:007,536.50 rejected (flat, unfinished — Poor High); balance 7,515–7,535; POC builds at 7,521.25The rally stopped at yesterday's structure without repairing it — recovery, not reversal-to-trend; two-sided acceptance forms around 7,521.25
15:00–16:00Mild MOC-window fade; RTH settles 7,526.75, above POCClose location Inside VA > POC — the day's last vote went to buyers (MOC imbalance itself not provided — unweighted)
16:00–17:45Post-close bleed to 7,518.25 as strike headlines hitThe overnight tape has already re-opened the question the RTH close answered — this is the ETH battlefield
Open-type verdict & what it pre-loads for tonightWednesday was a gap-and-go down that converted into a look-below-and-fail — a gap-trap reversal. The failure happened AT structure (the shelf), on peak fear (VIX 18.90 top-tick), which makes the low high-quality. But the recovery also STOPPED at structure (7,536.50/7,537.00) leaving a poor high — so neither side finished. A double-unfinished bracket day statistically resolves by repairing one extreme early in the next session; which one gets repaired first is the whole overnight/Thursday game, and the headline tape picks it.

STRUCTURE MAP (last vs profile) 7,605.50 drawn + Prev POC 7,604.25 + 2D Weak High 7,602.50 + 2D VAH 7,600.50 ◄ SUPPLY BLOCK 7,591.50 drawn 7,588.00 Tue RTH SELLING TAIL (zone 7,571.50→7,588) 7,578.25 drawn 7,571.50 yVAH ┐ 7,566.25 yPOC ┼ 7,565.00 drawn (red) — supply shelf 7,550.50 drawn 7,543.00 yVAL ◄ top of the VALUE-GAP SEAM ░░ 6-pt value gap ░░ 7,537.00 Wed VAH + POOR HIGH 7,536.50 ◄ bottom of seam 7,527.50 drawn 7,526.75 Wed RTH close 7,521.25 Wed POC ES 7,518.25 ►────────────────────────7,517.25 daily 20-EMA (the gate) 7,508.25 drawn 7,499.50 Wed VAL 7,482.25 drawn (fast zone 7,482→7,508 from the V-recovery) 7,474.00 daily P-shelf (1.101M) ┐ 7,469.50 POOR LOW + drawn ┴ THE FORK 7,455.50 Prev VAL 7,448.50 drawn green zone 7,443.50 Prev BUYING TAIL 7,439.25 daily 50-EMA 7,421.25 drawn 7,406.50 Prev VAL
Why the 7,537→7,543 seam is the single most important overhead structureWednesday's value TOP (7,537.00) and Tuesday's value BOTTOM (7,543.00) do not overlap — a 6-pt gap of non-acceptance sits between the two days' business, capped by the unfinished 7,536.50 poor high. Auctions hate seams: a push into 7,536.50 that repairs the poor high and then holds ABOVE 7,543.00 stitches the two value areas together and mechanically re-opens all of Tuesday's range (7,550.50 → 7,566.25 → 7,571.50). A push that tags 7,536.50 and fails leaves the seam intact and sends price back through the whole Wed value. One zone, binary outcome — it gates every bull scenario in this plan.
Why the poor low is a FORK, not a levelA poor/flat low at 7,469.50 is unfinished business — auctions revisit them to "repair" the low with excess. But repair has two endings: (1) the revisit dips under, finds no sellers, and reverses = look-below-and-fail, one of the highest-quality long entries in the playbook (that is literally what happened Wednesday morning); or (2) the revisit ACCEPTS below — 30-min value building under 7,469.50 — which converts the entire 7,469.50–7,474.00 shelf into overhead supply and opens the vacuum to 7,455.50 → 7,443.50–7,448.50 → the 50-EMA. Never trade the touch; trade the resolution.

Narrative: two consecutive lower-value days establish the corrective direction, but the internal quality diverges — Tuesday closed below its POC (sellers finishing strong), Wednesday closed above its POC (buyers finishing strong). That deceleration, plus a defended shelf and an unrepaired poor high directly overhead, describes a market that WANTS to balance-and-repair upward but is trading inside a headline regime that can gap it either way overnight. The overnight auction inherits the bracket: 7,469.50–7,536.50 is the field of play until one end accepts beyond.

d) VIX + macro regime — orderly repricing inside a hike-risk tape

InputReadingImplication
VX1! (front VIX future)18.20 flat (+0.11%) · range 17.57–18.90Orderly vol went NOWHERE on a down day with live strikes — lean less bearish
18.90 double rejection~04:30 headline spike + 10:30 cash flushFear capped the VIX top-tick printed on the exact ES low bar and was sold — panic was faded, not bought
Cash VIX16.90 +4.77%Sub-17 remarkably contained for a shooting war; term structure not in stress
Late tickVX1! 18.25 at 17:00 as strikes resumedETH risk live the overnight can re-price fast on a single headline
Fed / ratesMinutes 2:00 ET: neutral two-way; minority sees a hike case; funds 3.50–3.75%Inversion regime hot inflation / oil spike = SELL equities, soft data / de-escalation = BUY
OilWTI ~$74 +5%, Brent $76+; Hormuz threat "severe"The transmission oil is how Iran headlines become Fed policy risk
CalendarThu 8:30 ET Initial Claims (prior 215K, est ~220K); CPI Tue Jul 14Minor print claims rarely moves ES >10 pts alone — but on this tape any excuse can trigger the fork
Internals (Wed)SPX −0.28% · Dow −1.09% · Nasdaq +0.20%Narrow tech absorbed the selling; breadth was poor (67% decliners) — a tech-carried tape is headline-fragile

Narrative: the volatility market is the most bullish input on the board — it refused to expand on a war-headline down day and top-ticked at the price low. But the RATES regime is the most bearish: with a Fed that has removed its easing bias and a minority openly arguing for hikes, every barrel of oil upside is equity downside. The overnight is therefore asymmetric-by-headline: de-escalation news has a clean runway (vol already refuses to bid), escalation news hits a market with no Fed put underneath it. Trade the levels, quarter size, and let the headlines pick the branch.

04What-If Scenarios — ETH (E1–E6)

Overnight reads against Wednesday's profile until ON value develops. Headline tape: every probability below is conditional on no new tape-bomb; sizing is quarter-size max all night (see Playbook). Reaction function: escalation/oil-spike = SELL, de-escalation = BUY (hike-risk Fed inversion).
E1
Balance drift — overnight holds the 7,508.25–7,527.50 box
30% · base
▶
Zone
7,508.25–7,527.50, orbiting the 7,521.25 POC and the 20-EMA 7,517.25
Context
Absent a fresh headline, overnight inventory has no reason to move a coiled, mid-value tape: the RTH close above POC says longs are comfortable, the −8 post-close drift says shorts already pressed once, and Globex liquidity is too thin to force acceptance beyond either value edge without news. Asia/Europe digest the same strike headlines that were already sold-then-bought Wednesday.
Setup
Fade the box edges at quarter size only: buy 7,508.25–7,510 (stop 7,503, under VAL air), sell 7,526–7,527.50 (stop 7,532). Targets: the POC 7,521.25 from both sides. Skip entirely if headlines are flowing.
Confirm
VX1! pinned ≤18.5; 30-min ETH bars overlapping with no range extension beyond 7,530/7,505 through the Asia session
Invalidate
Any 30-min ETH close outside 7,499.50/7,536.50 — rolls into E3/E2 (down) or E5 (up)
Probability
★★★★ 30% — the modal overnight is quiet-until-it-isn't; the box held from 16:00–17:45 even WITH strike headlines printing, which is the tell that E1 starts as the path of least resistance
E2
Headline flush to 7,499.50 VAL — and it HOLDS
20% · ★★★
▶
Zone
7,499.50–7,508.25 test from above
Context
Strike footage or an oil pop knocks ES out of the box to the value floor. 7,499.50 is the natural first stop: it is Wednesday's accepted floor, the round 7,500, and the last defense before the fast 7,482–7,508 zone. In an orderly-VIX tape (18.90 already rejected twice), the first VAL test overnight is more often bought than accepted through — thin-tape probes lack the volume to build value below.
Setup
Responsive buy 7,500–7,504 after the first 5-min rejection wick; stop 7,494 (below VAL with buffer); targets 7,508.25 → 7,517–7,521.25; runner off. Quarter size.
Confirm
VX1! fails to take 18.90; the flush bar closes back above 7,502; no follow-through headline within 30 min
Invalidate
30-min ETH close below 7,499.50 — rolls into E3 (fork test) and flips the overnight lean bearish
Probability
★★★ 20% — headlines are literally live, so a VAL test is likely; the HOLD is favored over acceptance-below because VIX behavior says fear is being faded and the shelf below already repelled two attacks in 24 hours
E3
VAL breaks — fast trip through 7,482.25 to the 7,469.50–7,474 fork
16% · ★★★
▶
Zone
7,469.50–7,499.50, ending AT the fork
Context
Acceptance below 7,499.50 puts price back into the zone Wednesday repriced in minutes — the 7,482→7,508 climb was so fast it left no resting business, so the reverse trip is equally fast. The destination is pre-determined: the unfinished 7,469.50 poor low sitting on the 7,474.00 shelf. This is the poor-low repair trade arriving overnight instead of in RTH. The FORK then decides everything: look-below-and-fail = E-series long; acceptance below = E4.
Setup
No trade DURING the elevator ride. At the fork: buy the reclaim of 7,474 after a probe to 7,465–7,469 that fails to build (stop 7,461); targets 7,482.25 → 7,499.50. Or stand down and let RTH resolve it (preferred pre-9:30).
Confirm
For the long: single probe, immediate 5-min reclaim, VX1! rejected again at/under 19
Invalidate
30-min ETH close below 7,469.50 with value building under — rolls into E4
Probability
★★★ 16% — requires a real headline escalation (not just drift) to force acceptance out of value in thin trade; conditional on E2's floor failing, and the floor has been strong
E4
Fork ACCEPTS below — overnight trend-down to 7,455.50 → 7,439–7,448
12% · ★★
▶
Zone
Below 7,469.50: 7,455.50 → 7,448.50 → 7,443.50 → 7,439.25 (50-EMA)
Context
This is the escalation branch: a major overnight event (Hormuz closure headline, US casualties, oil gapping) forces 30-min value to build UNDER the shelf. The 7,469.50–7,474 zone converts to supply, the vacuum to 7,455.50 opens, and the deep-floor cluster (Prev Buying Tail 7,443.50 + green zone 7,448.50 + 50-EMA 7,439.25) becomes the magnet. Note the asymmetry: proven aggressive buyers live at 7,443.50 — trend-down overnight into that cluster historically finds responsive defense.
Setup
Sell the RETEST of 7,469.50–7,474 from below (stop 7,479); targets 7,455.50 → 7,448.50, final 7,443.50. Do NOT chase the break bar. Quarter size, hard stop, this is a headline tape.
Confirm
VX1! through 19 and holding; oil making new session highs; a 30-min close below 7,469.50 BEFORE entry
Invalidate
Reclaim of 7,474 on a 30-min close — the fork resolved long after all; flips back to E3's buy side
Probability
★★ 12% — real but tail-ish: it needs the ONE input that hasn't appeared yet (vol expansion). Every prior attack on the shelf died at a rejected VIX high; E4 requires that pattern to break
E5
Relief bid — reclaim 7,527.50, repair the 7,536.50 poor high, stall at the seam
16% · ★★★
▶
Zone
7,527.50–7,543.00
Context
A quiet-to-positive overnight (no new strikes, oil fades, Asia bid) lets the unfinished poor high exert its pull: 7,536.50 is only ~18 pts overhead and repair-magnets get repaired fast in calm tape. The test is what happens AFTER repair — the 7,537→7,543 seam is where Tuesday's sellers defend. Overnight tape rarely has the volume to force acceptance through a seam, so the base expectation is repair-then-stall, handing the decision to Thursday RTH.
Setup
Momentum long on the 7,527.50 reclaim (stop 7,521); targets 7,536.50 (scale most) → 7,541; flat into 7,543 — do not carry a seam-break bet in Globex. Quarter size.
Confirm
VX1! sinking toward 17.5; no strike headlines for 2+ hours; Europe opens bid
Invalidate
Failure back under 7,521.25 POC — rolls to E1; or a tape-bomb → E2/E3
Probability
★★★ 16% — the magnet is real and close, but it needs the headline flow to go quiet, which is a coin-flip at best with strikes announced for "tonight"
E6
De-escalation headline — seam breaks overnight, run to 7,550.50 → 7,565–7,571.50
6% · ★
▶
Zone
Above 7,543.00: 7,550.50 → 7,565.00/7,566.25 → 7,571.50
Context
The mirror of E4: a genuine de-escalation headline (talks announced, strikes paused, Hormuz reopening) in the inversion regime is a BUY with a clean runway — VIX is already refusing to expand, positioning is short-tilted after two down days, and above the seam there is a 23-pt air pocket to Tuesday's POC. Gap-and-go dynamics apply: the move happens in one impulse, then holds.
Setup
Buy the FIRST pullback that holds 7,543–7,545 from above (stop 7,536); targets 7,550.50 → 7,565.00, runner 7,571.50. Never chase the headline bar itself.
Confirm
VX1! breaking 17.57 (the day low); the seam retest holds within one 30-min bar
Invalidate
Re-entry below 7,537 — headline faded, seam intact; back to E5's stall
Probability
★ 6% — the White House said "hit them hard again tonight"; de-escalation before Thursday's open is the least likely branch, but it is the branch with the most travel, which is why it is written down

05Probability Matrix — ETH

RankScenarioDirectionZone★Weight
1E1 Balance drift in the boxNeutral7,508.25–7,527.50★★★★30%
2E2 VAL test holdsNeutral-bull7,499.50–7,508.25★★★20%
3E3 Fork test (7,469.50–7,474)Bear → fork7,469.50–7,499.50★★★16%
3E5 Poor-high repair, seam stallBull7,527.50–7,543.00★★★16%
5E4 Fork accepts below — trend-downBear<7,469.50 → 7,439–7,455★★12%
6E6 De-escalation seam breakBull>7,543 → 7,565–7,571.50★6%
Combined directional weightBull 30% (E2 hold-half + E5 + E6) · Neutral 37% · Bear 33% (E3 + E4 + E2-fail tail)
Base case defended: the overnight is scored nearly flat — 30 Bull / 37 Neutral / 33 Bear — because the five pillars split cleanly. AGAINST risk: value has migrated lower two straight days, the catalyst regime is hike-risk with strikes literally announced for tonight, and post-close drift is already −8. FOR risk: Wednesday's close location (Inside VA > POC after a defended shelf), a VIX that top-ticked at the price low and went out flat, and a fully intact rising MA stack. MOC imbalance was not provided and is unweighted. Neutral gets the plurality because thin Globex tape rarely resolves a double-poor bracket without a forcing headline — and if the headline comes, it is modestly more likely hostile (hence Bear 33 > Bull 30). To falsify: a de-escalation headline flips this to 55+ Bull instantly; a VX1! break over 19 with oil at highs flips it to 55+ Bear.

17:50 ET Wed — ES 7,518.25, strikes resuming, VX1! 18.20
│
├─ No fresh tape-bomb (Asia/Europe quiet)
│   ├─ Holds 7,508.25–7,527.50 box ──────────────► E1 balance — fade edges to POC, quarter size
│   └─ Calm bid develops → reclaims 7,527.50 ────► E5 repair 7,536.50 → stall 7,537–7,543 seam → hand off to RTH

│

    └─ 30-min close < 7,499.50 ─────────────────► E3 elevator through 7,482.25 to the FORK 7,469.50–7,474
         ├─ Probe fails, reclaims 7,474 ─────────► look-below-and-fail long → 7,482.25 → 7,499.50
         └─ 30-min value builds BELOW 7,469.50 ──► E4 trend-down → 7,455.50 → 7,448.50 → 7,443.50/7,439.25

Level-by-level — holds vs breaks (overnight)

LevelIf HOLDSIf BREAKS

Standing ETH rules tonight: quarter-size ceiling all night (headline tape); no new position within 15 min AFTER any Iran headline (let the first spike print — first-move inversion risk is high in this regime); be flat or quarter-size into the 8:30 ET claims print and the 9:30 cash open re-anchor. MOC not provided — unweighted.

Position size vs ATR — overnightATR 94.25 on a headline tape: quarter-size ceiling on EVERYTHING tonight, one position at a time, hard stops in the book (not mental). A single Iran headline can traverse the entire 7,469→7,543 field in minutes. If two ETH trades stop out, done until the 9:30 re-anchor.

09Structural Warnings — ETH

The FORK is not a level — 7,469.50–7,474.00An unfinished poor low ON a 1.101M volume shelf. Repair-reclaim = one of the best longs on the board; 30-min acceptance below = trend-down trigger to 7,455.50 → 7,439–7,448. Decide by RESOLUTION, never by touch. Trading the touch is how this zone punishes both sides.
Fast zone 7,482 → 7,508Wednesday's V-recovery repriced this band in minutes — it holds almost no resting business. Entries INSIDE it have no structure to lean on; expect elevator speed in both directions. Trade the edges (7,482.25 / 7,508.25), never the middle.
Poor high 7,536.50 + the 7,537→7,543 value-gap seamRepair magnet directly overhead, but the seam above it is Tuesday's sellers' line. The trap: buying the poor-high repair breakout and holding into the seam rejection. Repair is a TARGET overnight, not a breakout signal.
Selling tail 7,571.50–7,588.00Aggressive sellers stamped Tuesday's high. Any overnight miracle rally into this band is a fade until proven otherwise (acceptance above 7,588 in RTH, not ETH).
Event risk — live and layeredUS strikes announced for TONIGHT (any hour); oil the transmission channel to a hike-risk Fed; 8:30 ET claims Thursday (minor, prior 215K); June CPI Tue Jul 14 (the true binary). First move after any headline carries inversion risk — do not trade the spike.
No single prints labeled todayWednesday's profile carries poor extremes and prior-day tails, but no fresh single-print callouts — the speed-zone risk tonight is the drawn 7,482–7,508 fast zone, not a TPO single print. Flagged so nothing is assumed that the chart doesn't show.

10Session Transition — where ETH hands off to Thursday RTH

If price is here at ~9:20 ET ThuImplicationLikely RTH day type
7,508.25–7,537.00 (inside Wed upper value)Balance held — no overnight resolution; both poor extremes still liveRotational / repair day — RTH R1/R3 rules
7,537.00–7,543.00 (in the seam)Poor high repaired overnight; seam being contested at the openUpside range-extension candidate — RTH R2 gate
> 7,543.00 (above the seam)Value stitched to Tuesday's — gap-and-go up profile WITH the close-location signalTrend-up / open-drive risk — do not fade early
7,482.25–7,499.50 (below Wed VAL)Look-below risk at the open; fork test likely in the first hourOpen-rejection-reverse vs continuation — let IB decide (R4 vs R5)
7,455.50–7,469.50 (at/below the fork)Overnight already accepted below the shelf — gap extends the weak-side signalTrend-down risk — R5/R6 rules; first 30-min open-drive = continuation
< 7,448.50 (in/below the deep floor)Escalation shock gap >0.7×ATR — exhaustion-reversal vs liquidation fork at the 50-EMAGap-and-go down OR violent reversal — R6; half the field's odds, double its speed
Gap tiltWednesday CLOSED above its POC (strong-side signal) — so a gap UP Thursday travels WITH the close-location signal (continuation odds up, fill odds down), while a gap DOWN fights it (fill/reversion odds up, exactly like Wednesday's gap-trap). Measure any gap vs ATR 94.25: under ~28 pts = noise, treat as inside-value open.

11Final Summary — ETH

DimensionCall
Macro biasBull trend full rising MA stack, 2.3% off ATH — but hike-risk Fed + war overlay caps conviction
Intermediate biasCorrective value lower ×2, three days down off the 7,602.50 weak high
Overnight biasNeutral 30 Bull / 37 Neutral / 33 Bear — headline coin-flip inside a double-poor bracket
Base caseE1 box balance 7,508.25–7,527.50; first excursion more likely down (VAL test) than up
Primary tradeE2 responsive buy 7,500–7,504 first VAL test, stop 7,494, targets 7,508.25 → 7,521.25
Best-location alternateE3 look-below-and-fail long at the fork: reclaim of 7,474, stop 7,461
Real shortONLY the E4 retest sell 7,469.50–7,474 from below after a 30-min acceptance close, VX1! > 19

Key bear level7,469.50 — acceptance below converts the shelf and opens 7,455.50 → 7,439–7,448

01Top-Down Analysis — Thu Jul 09 RTH

Thursday opens with claims at 8:30 ET (minor), the Iran overnight in the books, and a double-poor Wednesday bracket demanding repair. The cash session's job: resolve the 7,469.50–7,536.50 field.

a) Daily — Thursday's close decides the 20-EMA gate

Daily: price on the 20-EMA 7,517.25 · rising 20>50>100>200>300 stack · P-shelf 7,474.00 (1.101M) under the poor low · ATH 7,694.75 · ATR 94.25
MAValueDistance (vs 7,518.25)Status
20-EMA7,517.25+1.00ON THE LINE Wed's close held it by <2 pts — the third straight session hugging the gate; Thursday's CLOSE is the verdict
50-EMA7,439.25+79.00HOLDING rising, embedded in the 7,439–7,456 deep-floor cluster
100-EMA7,296.25+222.00HOLDING rising
200-EMA7,091.25+427.00HOLDING rising
300-EMA6,921.00+597.25HOLDING rising
Daily drawn levelTypeDistanceNote
7,694.75ATH / High+176.50The ceiling; only in play on a multi-day de-escalation rally
7,474.00P — volume shelf (1.101M)−44.25🔗 Fused with the 7,469.50 poor low = the RTH fork; the single most consequential downside zone Thursday
7,277.00V — value node−241.25Near the 100-EMA — the crash-scenario reference only
6,943.75 and belowLower P/V ladder−574+Composite structure, out of Thursday's field
Why Thursday's close vs 7,517.25 is the trend verdictThe rising 20-EMA has defined this entire leg — every prior tag was bought within a session or two. Three consecutive closes pinned to the line means the dip-buyers and the sellers are at maximum tension exactly here. A Thursday close meaningfully ABOVE (say >7,530, back over the POC) re-arms the dip-buy regime; the first daily CLOSE below ~7,510 would be the first closing loss of the gate since this leg began — that is what converts "correction in an uptrend" into "trend test," and it would flip Friday's plan defensive regardless of intraday noise.
Why the 7,474.00 shelf is where the trend physically lives1.101M contracts is the deepest nearby volume node on the daily. Wednesday proved it: an open-drive with a war headline died there to the tick. If Thursday accepts BELOW it, the trapped volume becomes overhead supply and the road to the 50-EMA cluster (7,439–7,456) is short and thin. Holding it keeps every drawdown a "repair visit."

Narrative: nothing about the daily trend has broken — the stack is rising and untested below the 50-EMA. What Thursday decides is whether this three-day compression onto the gate resolves as the usual coiled-spring dip-buy (the pattern of the whole leg) or as the first genuine distribution under a hike-risk Fed. The candle to watch for: a second consecutive long-lower-shadow day would confirm accumulation at the shelf; a full-body close under the gate confirms the sellers finally got acceptance.

b) 5-min price-action recap + open type — what Wednesday's tape teaches Thursday

5-min Jul 8: the gap-trap template — drive into the shelf, VIX top-tick rejection, V-recovery, poor-high stall, above-POC settle
Time (ET)BeatWhat Thursday inherits from it
09:30 openGap-down 7,507 (0.49×ATR, with Tue's weak close) → open-drive to 7,482Proof the tape WILL open-drive on this news backdrop — respect the first 30 min, classify before trading
10:15–10:30Flush terminates at 7,469.50, flat low, VIX 18.90 top-tick soldThe shelf is defended and PRICED — Thursday sellers must beat a level that has now repelled three attacks (ETH probe, RTH drive, post-close drift)
10:45–12:30V-recovery: 7,482.25 / 7,500 / 7,508.25 reclaimed without pullbackThe 7,482–7,508 band is a speed zone — Thursday stops placed inside it will be skipped; place beyond the edges
12:30Push dies at 7,536.50 — flat, unfinished (Poor High), exactly at the developing VAHThe repair magnet AND the first sell test Thursday morning; a 9:45–10:30 repair attempt is the highest-frequency opening play
13:00–16:00Balance 7,515–7,535, POC 7,521.25, settle 7,526.75 above POCTwo-sided acceptance built ABOVE the midpoint — initiative sellers lost the afternoon; opening drives down start against resting length
16:00–17:45Drift −8 to 7,518.25 on resumed-strike headlinesOvernight inventory is modestly SHORT vs settle — an unchanged-to-up open squeezes it immediately (fuel for R1)
Open-type framework for Thursday (pre-computed)Classify the 9:30 print against Wed's range/value: inside 7,499.50–7,537.00 = balance rules, fade extremes toward 7,521.25 until a VA edge breaks. Outside value inside range (7,469.50–7,499.50 or 7,537.00–7,536.50 band) = look-above/below-and-fail watch. Beyond range with a <~28-pt gap (0.3×ATR) = noise, treat as inside. Gap >~47 pts (0.5×ATR): DOWN fights Wednesday's strong close → fill/reversion favored (Wednesday's own template); UP travels WITH the close signal → gap-and-go, hold-of-open is the tell, do not fade the first 30 min.

c) TPO Market Profile — the frame Thursday must resolve

TPO: Wed VA 7,499.50/7,521.25/7,537.00, close 7,526.75 Inside VA > POC · Poor High 7,536.50 / Poor Low 7,469.50 · Tue VA 7,543–7,571.50, POC 7,566.25, Selling Tail 7,588 · 2D Weak High 7,602.50
MetricYesterday (Tue Jul 7)Today (Wed Jul 8)

VAL7,543.007,499.50
Close LocInside VA < POCInside VA > POC
VA ShiftLowerLower
SessionCharacterValue / featuresMigration

Tue Jul 7Liquidation (chips + oil)VA 7,543–7,571.50 · POC 7,566.25 · close 7,553 < POC · Selling Tail 7,588Lower
Wed Jul 8Gap-trap reversalVA 7,499.50–7,537 · POC 7,521.25 · close 7,526.75 > POC · Poor High 7,536.50 / Poor Low 7,469.50Lower — decelerating (close loc flipped above POC)
Prior week (unrepaired refs)Standing untouched levelsPoor Highs 7,674 / 7,648.75 · Weak High 7,636.75 · POCs 7,666.50 / 7,604.25 · VAH 7,623.50 · VALs 7,455.50 / 7,406.50 · Buying Tail 7,443.50Open refs
THURSDAY'S FIELD 7,600.50–7,605.50 SUPPLY BLOCK (2D VAH/weak high + Prev POC + drawn) 7,588.00 Selling Tail top 7,571.50 yVAH — tail zone base 7,565.00–7,566.25 supply shelf (drawn red + yPOC) 7,550.50 drawn 7,543.00 yVAL — SEAM TOP ░░ 6-pt value gap ░░ 7,537.00 Wed VAH / POOR HIGH 7,536.50 — SEAM BOTTOM 7,527.50 drawn · 7,526.75 settle 7,521.25 Wed POC open ref 7,518.25 ►──────────────────7,517.25 daily 20-EMA gate 7,508.25 drawn 7,499.50 Wed VAL 7,482.25 drawn — fast zone 7,469.50–7,474.00 THE FORK (poor low + P-shelf 1.101M) 7,455.50 Prev VAL 7,439.25–7,448.50 DEEP FLOOR (50-EMA + tail 7,443.50 + zone) 7,421.25 → 7,406.50 → 7,396.50 → 7,366.25
Why the seam (7,537→7,543) is Thursday's bull gateWednesday's recovery stopped at the seam bottom and built its POC beneath it — meaning every buyer from the V-recovery is unrealized above 7,537. Repairing the poor high tags their breakeven-hope zone; ACCEPTING above 7,543 flips them from hopeful to funded and mechanically re-opens Tuesday's entire untraded range (23 clean points to the 7,565–7,571.50 shelf). This one 6-pt band converts a repair-bounce into a range-extension day. Every bull scenario routes through it.
Why the fork (7,469.50–7,474) is Thursday's bear gate — and a fork, not a levelThe market owes the poor low a visit; the only question is which resolution. Look-below-and-fail (probe, no acceptance, reclaim) = the same A+ long that printed Wednesday, now with an even more proven shelf. Acceptance below (30-min value under 7,469.50) = the shelf's 1.1M contracts become supply, and the 7,455.50 → 7,439–7,448 vacuum trades fast. Direction of resolution is set by the overnight news and the VIX's willingness to finally hold above 19.

Narrative: the two-day value migration is lower, but its second derivative turned — Tuesday's sellers finished in control (close < POC), Wednesday's did not (close > POC after a defended flush). Auctions that decelerate INTO major support with unfinished business on both ends typically repair the near extreme first (7,536.50 is 18 pts away; the fork is 49). That geometry — plus overnight inventory short vs settle — makes an early poor-high repair the highest-frequency opening sequence, with the seam then deciding whether Thursday becomes a range-extension day or another rotation back through value.

d) VIX + macro — the regime Thursday trades inside

InputReadingImplication
VX1!18.20 flat · 18.90 rejected at the ES low, twiceOrderly repricing vol top-ticked at the price low = fear faded, not bought — lean less bearish in the weights
Cash VIX16.90 +4.77%Contained sub-17 with live strikes; no stress in term structure
Fed regimeMinutes: neutral two-way, minority hike case; ~20% July-hike pricing; CPI Jul 14Inversion active oil/hot data = SELL, de-escalation/soft data = BUY — no Fed put under the tape
Oil / IranWTI ~$74 +5% · strikes resumed Wed evening · Hormuz "severe"The master variable Thursday's open location is Iran's overnight choice
Thu calendar8:30 ET Initial Claims (prior 215K, est ~220K)Minor a 10K miss either way is noise UNLESS it lands on a fork/seam test — then it's the trigger
InternalsWed: Nasdaq +0.2% vs 67% decliners; chips wobbling (Samsung, SMH −4.5% Tue)Narrow tech leadership a chip headline can move ES like a macro print this week

Narrative: Thursday's regime is "no Fed put, but no fear either." The vol market keeps refusing to pay up for war risk — that is the strongest standing argument against chasing shorts into support. The rates market keeps refusing to price cuts — that is the strongest standing argument against chasing longs into supply. The result is a fade-the-extremes regime between the fork and the supply shelf until a headline genuinely changes one of the two refusals.

TARGET MAP — Thursday Upside ladder: 7,521.25 → 7,527.50 → 7,536.50/7,537 → 7,543 → 7,550.50 → 7,565/7,566.25 → 7,571.50 → 7,578.25 → 7,588 → 7,591.50 → 7,600.50–7,605.50 → 7,621.75/7,623.50 → 7,632.25 → 7,636.75 → 7,648.75 → 7,654.50 → 7,666.50–7,674 → 7,694.75 ATH.
Downside ladder: 7,508.25 → 7,499.50 → 7,482.25 → 7,469.50–7,474 → 7,455.50 → 7,448.50 → 7,443.50 → 7,439.25 → 7,421.25 → 7,406.50 → 7,396.50 → 7,366.25 → 7,350 → 7,320.
Five-star deciders: the seam 7,536.50–7,543 · the shelf 7,565–7,566.25 · the block 7,600.50–7,605.50 · the fork 7,468.50–7,474 · the deep floor 7,439.25–7,448.50.

03Context Summary — what Thursday RTH opens into

30-min RTH+ETH: the three-day corrective leg off 7,602.50 into a defended shelf · Thursday opens inside the double-poor bracket 7,469.50–7,536.50 under the value-gap seam
DimensionReadingImplication
Prior day typeGap-trap reversal (gap-and-go down → look-below-and-fail)Reversal days that close above POC lean follow-through UP the next morning — but only until the seam
Settlement location7,526.75 — above POC, upper half of valueConstructive
Profile shapeDouble-distribution recovery, poor at BOTH extremesBracketed — repair trade dominates the first 90 min
Value migrationLower ×2 but overlapping, and close location flipped above POCCorrective, decelerating — not trend-down yet
Price vs POC/VAH/VAL7,518.25: POC −3 · VAH −18.75 · VAL +18.75Dead-center — the open print, not the overnight, gives the edge
Extension from POC~3 ptsNo mean-reversion tension
Daily MA contextOn the 20-EMA 7,517.25; 50-EMA +79 below inside the deep floorGate day — the 16:00 close is the deliverable
ATR / expected move94.25 — one normal day spans fork→supply shelfHalf-size first trade, full only after one clean 30-min confirm
CatalystIran overnight + claims 8:30 (minor) + hike-risk Fed; CPI Jul 14 loomingInversion regime — headlines pick the branch, levels price it
Internals / flowsNarrow tech leadership; 67% decliners Wed; MOC not provided — unweightedFragile breadth

04What-If Scenarios — Thu RTH (R1–R6)

Keyed to the 9:30 open location vs Wednesday's value (7,499.50 / 7,521.25 / 7,537.00). Reaction function: this is not a scheduled-binary day, but the unscheduled binary (Iran) is live — escalation/oil-up = SELL, de-escalation = BUY; claims 8:30 only matters if it lands ON a level test. Weights assume an inside-value open; re-anchor at 9:20 if the overnight resolved a poor extreme already.
R1
Inside open, POC holds — repair the 7,536.50 poor high, fight the seam
28% · base
▶
Zone
Open 7,508–7,537; action 7,517–7,543
Context
The highest-frequency path: overnight inventory is short vs the 7,526.75 settle, the close location was strong, and the nearest unfinished business (7,536.50) is only ~18 pts up. An unchanged open squeezes shorts into the repair magnet in the first hour. The real information arrives AT the seam: repair-and-hold above 7,537 sets up the R2 handoff; repair-and-fail leaves the seam intact and rotates back to POC — still a profitable R1 if entered at the dip, not the breakout.
Setup
Buy the first dip that holds 7,514–7,518 (20-EMA + open-print support) after the opening rotation confirms above 7,508.25; stop 7,506; targets 7,527.50 → 7,536.50 (scale most) → 7,541. Half size until one clean 30-min structure prints.
Confirm
First 30-min bar holds above 7,508.25; VX1! ≤18.5 and fading; claims in-line (~215–225K)
Invalidate
30-min close below 7,499.50 — rolls into R4's approach; or acceptance above 7,543 — upgrade to R2
Probability
★★★★ 28% — defended: close > POC (bull pillar), VIX orderly (bull), MA stack intact (bull) vs value-migration lower (bear) and the hike-risk catalyst (bear). Three pillars to two, but the two bear pillars cap it below a majority — hence repair-then-fight, not trend-up.
R2
Seam acceptance — range extension into Tuesday's field (7,550.50 → 7,565–7,571.50)
12% · ★★
▶
Zone
Above 7,543; targets 7,550.50 / 7,565.00–7,566.25 / 7,571.50, runner 7,588
Context
Requires the seam to STITCH: two 30-min closes above 7,543 (or a de-escalation gap-up straight through it — then gap-and-go rules, the open-hold is the tell). Once stitched, Tuesday's 23-pt untraded core to the yPOC is open air, and every trapped Tuesday short from 7,543–7,566 is fuel. The 7,565–7,571.50 shelf is where the day's initiative buying is graded: acceptance there turns Thursday into a genuine trend-up day toward the tail and the 7,600 block.
Setup
Buy the first pullback that holds 7,543–7,545 from above (stop 7,536); targets 7,550.50 (scale ⅓) → 7,565.00 (scale ⅓) → 7,571.50; runner only if VX1! < 17.57 (new lows) toward 7,588.
Confirm
Two consecutive 30-min closes > 7,543; oil off its highs; breadth positive (this cannot be a 5-stock rally through a seam)
Invalidate
Re-entry below 7,537 — look-above-and-fail; flip to R3's fade and expect a full rotation to POC/VAL
Probability
★★ 12% — the structure is ready but the regime isn't: stitching a seam against a hike-risk Fed with strikes overnight needs a positive catalyst, and none is scheduled. Priced as the de-escalation branch.
R3
Balance/rotation day — fade the value edges around 7,521.25
20% · ★★★
▶
Zone
7,499.50–7,537.00, POC 7,521.25 the hub
Context
The "nothing resolves" day: claims in-line, no Iran news either way, and the market builds a second distribution around Wednesday's POC while it waits for CPI. Both poor extremes survive another day (they can — repair is a tendency, not a law). Responsive trade at the edges is the only business: the poor-high tag gets sold (unfinished ≠ accepted), the VAL tag gets bought (value ≠ abandoned).
Setup
Sell 7,535–7,537 after a repair-tag stalls (5-min lower high; stop 7,541 — a seam poke), target 7,521.25 → 7,510. Buy 7,500–7,504 on the first VAL test (stop 7,494), target 7,517–7,521.25. Both half size.
Confirm
IB (first hour) contains both probes; VX1! pinned 17.8–18.5; volume light
Invalidate
Any 30-min acceptance beyond 7,543 (→R2) or below 7,499.50 (→R4/R5)
Probability
★★★ 20% — no-catalyst days default to balance, and dead-center price vs POC (no extension tension) plus a pre-CPI calendar argue for position-squaring rotation. Capped because two poor extremes rarely BOTH survive a full session.
R4
Look-below-and-fail at the FORK — the repair long
15% · ★★★
▶
Zone
Flush 7,499.50 → 7,469.50–7,474 probe; trade back through 7,482.25 → 7,499.50 → 7,521.25
Context
The poor low finally gets its repair visit — a soft open or a morning headline breaks 7,499.50, the fast zone elevators price to the shelf, the probe dips under 7,469.50… and fails to build value. This is Wednesday's template on a proven level, and it is the single best risk/reward long in the plan because the invalidation is structural and tight. The tell that it's R4 and not R5: the flush is FAST (speed = stop-runs, not distribution) and VIX cannot hold its spike.
Setup
Buy the 30-min reclaim of 7,474–7,476 after a probe to 7,462–7,469 rejects (stop 7,459); targets 7,482.25 (scale ⅓) → 7,499.50 (⅓) → 7,517–7,521.25 runner. Half size on entry, add ONLY above 7,482.25.
Confirm
Probe holds <2 consecutive 30-min bars below 7,469.50; VX1! spike over 19 REJECTED within the hour; delta divergence at the low
Invalidate
30-min VALUE building below 7,469.50 (not just prints) — that is R5; stand down and flip the book
Probability
★★★ 15% — needs the bear pillars (value migration, catalyst) to force the trip AND the bull pillars (VIX behavior, shelf defense record, MA stack) to win the resolution — exactly the split the evidence shows, which is why the fork's long side outweighs its short side by 15 to 15-with-R6-riding.
R5
Fork ACCEPTS below — trend-down to 7,455.50 → 7,439–7,448
15% · ★★★
▶
Zone
Below 7,469.50: 7,455.50 → 7,448.50 → 7,443.50 → 7,439.25; stretch 7,421.25
Context
The escalation session: overnight strikes worsened, oil gaps again, and the third attack on the shelf finally builds VALUE below it. The 1.1M-contract shelf flips to supply, longs from Wednesday's entire recovery are trapped, and the vacuum to 7,455.50 trades in minutes. Respect the deep-floor cluster: a rising 50-EMA plus a proven buying tail is where trend-down days go to die — shorts must be paid BEFORE it, not through it. A 16:00 close below the 20-EMA gate on this path also flips the daily read (see Top-Down a).
Setup
Do NOT chase the break. Sell the retest of 7,469.50–7,474 from below after the first 30-min acceptance close (stop 7,481); targets 7,455.50 (scale ½) → 7,448.50 (¼) → 7,443.50; flat by 7,439.25 unless VX1! > 20 and trending.
Confirm
30-min close below 7,469.50 with value migrating under; VX1! HOLDING > 19 (the missing ingredient so far); oil at new highs; breadth < 25% advancers
Invalidate
Reclaim of 7,474 on a 30-min close — the fork resolved long (R4); cover instantly, the same speed that paid you now works against you
Probability
★★★ 15% — the bear pillars are real (value lower ×2, inversion regime, live war) but every prior acceptance attempt failed on a rejected VIX; this branch is priced as "the pattern finally breaks," not the default.
R6
Escalation shock — gap-down open beyond the fork (open < 7,455)
10% · ★★
▶
Zone
Open below 7,455 (gap >0.7×ATR); field 7,396.50–7,455.50
Context
A genuine overnight shock (Hormuz closed, major escalation) opens Thursday already through the fork AND into/below the deep floor. Per the gap table this is trend-day risk OR exhaustion reversal, and the first 30-min IB decides: an open-drive lower = liquidation continuation toward 7,421.25 → 7,406.50/7,396.50; a look-below-and-fail at the 50-EMA/tail cluster = violent reversal (these gaps against a rising 50-EMA with proven buyers at 7,443.50 snap back hard). Note the gap DOWN fights Wednesday's strong close signal — fill odds are non-trivial even on bad news.
Setup
No pre-planned entry — classify first. Continuation: sell a failed retest of 7,455.50 from below (stop 7,463), targets 7,439 → 7,421.25. Reversal: buy the 30-min reclaim of 7,448.50 after the cluster holds (stop 7,436), targets 7,469.50 → 7,482.25. Quarter size either way, one attempt.
Confirm
Continuation: open-drive, VX1! > 21 and bid. Reversal: cluster probe rejected within IB, VX1! failing off the spike
Invalidate
Whichever branch's trigger level recrosses — flat, one re-entry maximum on a shock day
Probability
★★ 10% — "hit them hard again tonight" makes this the fattest tail in weeks; it stays a tail because oil, not equities, has been absorbing the war premium so far.

05Probability Matrix — Thu RTH

RankScenarioDirectionZone★Weight
1R1 Repair the poor high, fight the seamBull7,508–7,543★★★★28%
2R3 Balance/rotation around POCNeutral7,499.50–7,537★★★20%
3R4 Fork look-below-and-fail longBull (from below)7,462–7,521★★★15%
3R5 Fork accepts — trend-downBear7,421–7,469.50★★★15%
5R2 Seam acceptance — range extension upBull7,543–7,588★★12%
6R6 Escalation shock gap-downBear<7,455 open★★10%
Combined directional weightBull 55% (R1+R2+R4) · Neutral 20% (R3) · Bear 25% (R5+R6)
Base case defended — 55 Bull / 20 Neutral / 25 Bear: the bull side carries the plurality because THREE of the five evidence pillars point up from this location — close location (Inside VA > POC after a defended flush — buyers finished Wednesday in control), VIX behavior (18.90 top-ticked at the price low and was sold twice; vol refuses to pay for war risk = orderly repricing, the classic lean-less-bearish signal), and the MA stack (fully risen, untested 50-EMA, price ON a rising gate that has bought every prior tag). The two pillars pointing down — value migration (lower two straight days) and the catalyst regime (hike-risk Fed + live strikes + oil transmission) — are why Bull is 55 and not 70, why R1's objective is the seam rather than Tuesday's POC, and why every bull scenario is written as repair-then-prove rather than trend-up. MOC imbalance was not provided — unweighted. Falsifiers, stated plainly: 30-min VALUE below 7,469.50 with VX1! holding 19+ collapses the bull case to ~25% on the spot; two 30-min closes above 7,543 with breadth participating lifts it toward 70%. A genuine directional short does NOT exist above the fork in this plan — that requires the regime shift R5/R6 describe.

06Decision Map — Thu RTH

8:30 ET claims (minor; prior 215K) → 9:30 open — classify vs Wed value 7,499.50 / 7,521.25 / 7,537.00
│
├─ OPEN INSIDE VALUE (7,499.50–7,537) — balance rules
│   ├─ First 30-min holds > 7,508.25, dip holds 7,514–7,518 ──► R1 long → 7,527.50 → repair 7,536.50
│   │    ├─ Seam: two 30-min closes > 7,543 ─────────────────► R2 extension → 7,550.50 → 7,565–7,571.50 → 7,588
│   │    └─ Seam rejects, re-entry < 7,537 ──────────────────► R3 rotation — fade back to 7,521.25 / 7,510
│   └─ 30-min close < 7,499.50 ──────────────────────────────► fast zone opens → the FORK test
│        ├─ Probe < 7,469.50 fails, 30-min reclaims 7,474 ───► R4 repair long → 7,482.25 → 7,499.50 → 7,521.25
│        └─ 30-min VALUE builds < 7,469.50, VX1! holds 19+ ──► R5 trend-down — sell the retest → 7,455.50 → 7,439–7,448
│
├─ OPEN 7,537–7,543 or gap-up beyond ─ WITH Wed's strong close ─► gap-and-go up watch: hold of open = R2 rules, fail = look-above-and-fail → R3
│
└─ OPEN < 7,455 (shock gap > 0.7×ATR) ───────────────────────► R6 — let IB classify: open-drive = continuation to 7,421.25; cluster rejection = violent reversal long

Level-by-level — holds vs breaks (Thursday)

LevelIf HOLDSIf BREAKS

7,469.50–7,474.00 (FORK)DIP-BUY look-below-and-fail = the plan's A+ long (R4) → 7,482.25 → 7,499.50trend-down shelf converts, longs trapped → 7,455.50 → deep floor (R5)
7,455.50 (Prev VAL)responsive buy vacuum landing bought → back-test 7,469.50 from belowaccelerates deep-floor cluster next — pay shorts before it
7,439.25–7,448.50 (deep floor)responsive buy proven tail + rising 50-EMA — trend-down days die here → bounce to 7,455.50/7,469.50regime break 50-EMA lost intraday → 7,421.25 → 7,406.50/7,396.50; plan re-anchors bearish (R6 field)

Starting biasFlip toTriggerAction
Neutral-bullBullishTwo 30-min closes > 7,543.00Seam stitched — trade the R2 ladder; add on the 7,543–7,545 hold

Standing Thursday rules: half size on the FIRST trade of the day, full size only after one clean 30-min structure confirms the branch; no new risk 8:25–8:35 around claims; any Iran headline → 15-min no-trade window (inversion risk); the 16:00 close vs 7,517.25 is a deliverable — note it for Friday's re-anchor. MOC not provided — unweighted.

08Tactical Playbook — Thu RTH top 3

1
R1 — Opening dip-buy into the poor-high repair
R:R ~1:2.3
▶
Entry
7,514–7,518 on the first pullback AFTER the opening 30-min holds above 7,508.25 — the 20-EMA + open-print confluence; no entries before the IB gives structure
Stop
7,506 — under the shelf and the entry structure; a 30-min close < 7,508.25 says the balance is tilting, don't argue
Scaling
Half size in; ⅓ off 7,527.50, ⅓ off 7,535–7,536.50 (INTO the magnet), rest only on a confirmed seam hold > 7,543 targeting 7,550.50
Targets
7,527.507,536.507,5437,550.50
Kill switch
Iran escalation headline mid-trade → flatten, 15-min window; VX1! reclaiming 18.9 → tighten to breakeven
2
R4 — The fork repair long (best R:R in the plan)
R:R ~1:3.0
▶
Entry
7,474–7,476 on the 30-min reclaim after a probe to 7,462–7,469 fails to build value — patience: the reclaim, never the falling knife
Stop
7,459 — below the probe; if a second acceptance push prints, the fork is resolving short and R5 owns the tape
Scaling
Half size; ⅓ off 7,482.25, ⅓ off 7,499.50, runner 7,517–7,521.25; add above 7,482.25 only
Targets
7,482.257,499.507,521.25
Kill switch
VX1! holding above 19 for a full 30-min bar while in the trade → exit; the pattern this trade leans on has broken
3
R5 — Acceptance-below retest short (the only real directional short above the crash field)
R:R ~1:2.4
▶
Entry
7,469.50–7,474 retest FROM BELOW, only after a 30-min close under 7,469.50 with value migrating down — chasing the break bar into the vacuum is donating slippage
Stop
7,481 — back above the converted shelf kills the acceptance thesis
Scaling
Half size; ½ off 7,455.50, ¼ off 7,448.50, rest 7,443.50; flat by the 50-EMA 7,439.25 unless VX1! > 20 and trending
Targets
7,455.507,448.507,443.50
Kill switch
Any 30-min reclaim of 7,474 → cover everything instantly; look-below-and-fail reversals from this shelf move at elevator speed
Position size vs ATR — ThursdayATR 94.25: a single normal day covers the whole fork-to-shelf field. Half size on the first trade of the day; full size only after one clean 30-min structure confirms the branch; quarter size on anything taken within 30 minutes of an Iran headline; two stop-outs = done until the afternoon re-anchor. The 16:00 close vs the 20-EMA gate matters more than any single intraday fill.

09Structural Warnings — Thu RTH

The FORK — 7,468.50–7,474.00 — is a decision zone, not supportPoor low + 1.101M P-shelf. Wednesday's defense makes it look invincible; three defenses in 24 hours also mean everyone's stop lives under it. Resolution, not touch: R4 above the reclaim, R5 below the acceptance, nothing in between.
Fast zone 7,482 → 7,508 — speed both waysRepriced in minutes Wednesday; no resting business inside. Stops inside this band WILL be traded through with slippage. Enter/exit at the edges (7,482.25, 7,499.50, 7,508.25) only.
Poor high 7,536.50 + the seam — the breakout trapThe most likely Thursday head-fake: repair prints, breakout buyers pile in, and Tuesday's sellers at 7,537–7,543 hand them a full rotation back to POC. Repair is a TARGET; only two 30-min closes above 7,543 convert it into a signal.
Selling tail 7,571.50–7,588.00 + extension riskIf R2 runs, remember the day started 3 pts from POC — by 7,566 price is ~45 pts extended and entering proven aggressive supply. Late-day longs into the tail are donations without a de-escalation driver.
Structural voids — both directionsAbove: the 6-pt value gap 7,537→7,543 (price moves fast through non-acceptance). Below: the vacuum 7,469.50→7,455.50 and again 7,448.50→7,421.25. Voids are for TARGETING, not for initiating.
Event risk stackIran headlines at ANY hour (inversion: first move often wrong — 15-min rule); 8:30 claims (minor, but a big miss ON a level test becomes the trigger); hike-risk Fed with CPI Tue Jul 14 — expect position-squaring flows into the weekend leg. MOC imbalance not provided — unweighted; watch the 15:50 tape for the real institutional vote.
No fresh single prints labeledWednesday's chart carries no single-print callouts — the speed risk is the drawn fast zone, not TPO singles. Prior-week unrepaired magnets (7,602.50 / 7,636.75 / 7,648.75 / 7,674) remain overhead for the multi-day map.

10Session Transition — Thu open-location grid

If price is here at 9:30 ETImplicationLikely day type
7,508.25–7,537.00 (inside upper value)Balance preserved; short overnight inventory squeezes toward the repair magnetR1 repair morning → seam decides the afternoon
7,499.50–7,508.25 (lower value)Soft open under the fulcrum; VAL test first, repair secondR3 rotation with R4 risk if VAL fails
7,537.00–7,543.00 (in the seam)Poor high already repaired overnight — the seam fight IS the openR2 gate day — acceptance = extension, rejection = full rotation
> 7,543.00 (above the seam)Gap up WITH Wednesday's strong close — continuation odds elevated, fill odds cutGap-and-go / trend-up risk — hold-of-open is the tell; do not fade the first 30 min
7,469.50–7,499.50 (below value, inside range)Look-below open against the strong-close signal — Wednesday's own template says fill/reversion is liveORR / R4 fork morning — let the IB classify
7,455.50–7,469.50 (below the fork)Overnight accepted below the shelf — the weak branch arrived before the bellR5 trend-down risk — open-drive = continuation; sell retests, not breaks
< 7,455 (shock gap)>0.7×ATR gap into the deep floor — maximum two-sided violenceR6 — IB decides continuation vs exhaustion reversal at the 50-EMA cluster
Gap tilt restatedWednesday closed ABOVE its POC: gaps UP travel with the signal (trend risk), gaps DOWN fight it (fill risk — exactly how Wednesday itself resolved). Under ~28 pts (0.3×ATR) = noise → inside-value rules.

11Final Summary — Thu RTH

DimensionCall
Macro biasBull trend full rising stack, 2.3% off ATH — trend intact until the 20-EMA gate closes below
Intermediate biasCorrective value lower ×2 off the 7,602.50 weak high — but decelerating (close-loc flip)
Intraday biasNeutral-bull 55 Bull / 20 Neutral / 25 Bear — repair-then-prove, not trend-up
Base caseR1: inside open → dip-buy 7,514–7,518 → repair 7,536.50 → the seam decides the afternoon
Primary tradeR1 long 7,514–7,518, stop 7,506, scale 7,527.50 / 7,536.50, runner on a seam hold
Best-location alternateR4 fork repair long: 30-min reclaim of 7,474 after a failed probe, stop 7,459 — best R:R on the board

Key bull level7,543.00 — two 30-min closes above stitches value → 7,550.50 → 7,565–7,571.50 → 7,588
Key bear level7,469.50 — 30-min VALUE below converts the shelf → 7,455.50 → 7,439–7,448 (and watch the 16:00 close vs 7,517.25)
Biggest riskHeadline inversion whipsaw: buying the repair breakout into the seam, or shorting the fork touch that reclaims — both are the structure punishing impatience
Day-type expectationRotational-to-upside-repair day inside 7,469.50–7,543; range extension only on a seam stitch (up) or shelf acceptance (down)
